article.title: Topical Spray of dsRNA Induces Mortality and Inhibits Chilli Leaf Curl Virus Transmission by Bemisia tabaci Asia II 1

events.event_label: DNA-A component detected in chilli plants and identified as ChiLCV
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p035.s001: The identity of the virus was confirmed by sequencing the DNA-A component from infected chilli plants. PMC8909865.p035.s002: PCR amplification of the DNA-A using Begomo F and Begomo R primer produced a 2.7 kb product visualized on 1% agarose gel. PMC8909865.p035.s003: Bidirectional sequencing of the cloned products produced a 2763 nt sequence comprising complete DNA-A that showed 100% nucleotide identity to ChiLCV isolates upon BLASTn analysis. PMC8909865.p035.s004: The sequence can be retrieved by GenBank Accession No. OM513903.

events.event_label: Artificial feeding with dsRNA diet
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p158.s001: Effect of hsp70 and fas2 dsRNA delivered to Bemisia tabaci through artificial diet. PMC8909865.p158.s002: (a) Artificial feeding setup for B. tabaci adults. PMC8909865.p158.s003: The artificial diet was supplemented with different concentrations of dsRNA and sterile water (control). PMC8909865.p158.s004: (b) Percent mortality of adult B. tabaci after 24 h of hsp70 and fas2 dsRNA feeding at 1.0, 2.0, and 3.0 μg/mL in comparison to control.

events.event_label: mtCOI gene detected in B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p034.s001: A homogeneous population of B. tabaci developed from a single adult female was characterized by sequencing the mtCOI gene. PMC8909865.p034.s002: PCR amplification of B. tabaci mtCOI with C1-J-2195 and L2-N-3014 primers (Table 1) showed an expected amplicon of ~860 bp on an agarose gel. PMC8909865.p034.s003: The nucleotide (nt) sequence showed 99.99% homology in BLASTn analysis with other B. tabaci Asia II 1 sequences in NCBI. PMC8909865.p034.s004: The sequence can be retrieved using the GenBank Accession No. MT920041.

events.event_label: dsRNA spray induces mortality and inhibits virus transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p001.s001: Topical Spray of dsRNA Induces Mortality and Inhibits Chilli Leaf Curl Virus Transmission by Bemisia tabaci Asia II 1

events.event_label: dsRNA produced from hsp70 and fas2
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p037.s006: The fragments were cloned between two T7 promoters of L4440 RNAi vector (Addgene 1654 provided by Andrew Fire, Carnegie Institution for Science, Washington, DC, USA). PMC8909865.p037.s007: The recombinant plasmids were transformed into RNase III mutant Escherichia coli HT115 cells (provided by Caenorhabditis Genetics Center, Minneapolis, MN, USA). PMC8909865.p037.s008: Total RNA isolated from recombinant E. coli HT115 cells and the dsRNA purified from total RNA using DNase I and RNase A were visualized in agarose gel electrophoresis (Figure 1c). PMC8909865.p037.s009: The purified dsRNA of hsp70 and fas2 produced single specific bands of ~130 bp and ~150 bp on 2% native agarose gel (Figure 1c). PMC8909865.p037.s010: Upon bidirectional sequencing of the recombinant plasmids, both the sequences of hsp70 (Accession No. MZ158306) and fas2 (Accession No. MZ766125) showed 100% homology with B. tabaci hsp70 and fas2, respectively.

events.event_label: hsp70 and fas2 upregulated in viruliferous B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p052.s001: Hsp70 and fas2 were found to be significantly upregulated in viruliferous B. tabaci along with several other genes. PMC8909865.p052.s002: Gene regulatory networking showed these genes were enriched with higher degrees of interactions. PMC8909865.p052.s003: Hsp70 transcripts increased upon ingestion of tomato yellow leaf curl virus (TYLCV) and squash leaf curl virus (SLCV) in DNA microarray. PMC8909865.p052.s004: The role of hsp70 in begomovirus transmission is well studied.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA feeding reduces ChiLCV copies and inhibits transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p053.s006: The resistance to ChiLCV in B. tabaci was enhanced at higher concentrations of hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p053.s007: ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci were imperceptible when B. tabaci fed on 3 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA for 24 h. The transmission of ChiLCV from infected to healthy plants by B. tabaci Asia II 1 was completely inhibited at 3 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA treatment under controlled conditions. PMC8909865.p053.s008: Our findings are consistent with Kanakala et al., who reported the deleterious effect of B. tabaci hsp70 dsRNA on TYLCV transmission. PMC8909865.p053.s009: The TYLCV titer decreased in B. tabaci Middle East Asia Minor 1 (MEAM 1) after feeding on hsp70 dsRNA-expressing tomato plants. PMC8909865.p053.s010: Transmission of TYLCV also dropped by 12% post-silencing of hsp70.

events.event_label: ChiLCV infection upregulates fas2 in B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p053.s021: The Fas2 orthologue in mammals (NCAM) is known to serve as a receptor for the rabies virus. PMC8909865.p053.s022: Although NCAM promotes the penetration of the virus in cells, it suppresses virus replication via induction of Interferon-ß, which is mainly involved in innate immunity against viral infection. PMC8909865.p053.s023: Upregulation of fas2 transcripts in B. tabaci post-ChiLCV infection might be due to the innate immune response against the virus infection. PMC8909865.p053.s024: We hypothesized that fas2 has a negative regulatory role in ChiLCV infection, and thus, knocking down fas2 increased the virus titer in B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p053.s025: Hence, silencing fas2 was not considered to induce resistance to ChiLCV despite its efficacy in eradicating B. tabaci.

events.event_label: dsRNA treatment increases mortality and decreases hsp70 mRNA in B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p054.s009: Hence, naked hsp70 dsRNA without any delivery agent was topically sprayed onto the foliage of the ChiLCV-infected chilli plant under contained semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p054.s010: B. tabaci adults were released on the dsRNA-sprayed plants to evaluate the dsRNA’s efficacy in eradicating B. tabaci and inhibiting the transmission of ChiLCV. PMC8909865.p054.s011: Considering the extracellular degradation of dsRNA and delivery through the host plant, a 10-fold higher dose than the controlled assay was used for topical application. PMC8909865.p054.s012: A 67.77% mortality with a 4.6-fold decrease in hsp70 mRNA levels in B. tabaci exposed to the dsRNA-sprayed plants was noted after 24 h of release. PMC8909865.p054.s013: Further, 1.84 × 108-fold decreased ChiLCV copies and 75% reduced transmission of ChiLCV indicated the efficiency of topically sprayed hsp70 dsRNA in managing both the virus and its vector.

events.event_label: dsRNA treatment decreases ChiLCV copies and reduces transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p054.s012: A 67.77% mortality with a 4.6-fold decrease in hsp70 mRNA levels in B. tabaci exposed to the dsRNA-sprayed plants was noted after 24 h of release. PMC8909865.p054.s013: Further, 1.84 × 108-fold decreased ChiLCV copies and 75% reduced transmission of ChiLCV indicated the efficiency of topically sprayed hsp70 dsRNA in managing both the virus and its vector. PMC8909865.p054.s014: Although the efficacy of topically applied naked dsRNA against plant viruses is known, this is the first evidence of its efficacy against a hemipteran insect like B. tabaci.

events.event_label: hsp70 and fas2 genes produce siRNA
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p037.s001: A conserved 128 nt-long (2416 to 2543 nt) fragment of the hsp70 gene (~2.5 kb) and a 150 nt-long (1085 to 1234 nt) fragment of the fas2 gene (~1.4 kb) of B. tabaci were selected for dsRNA designing (Figure 1a,b). PMC8909865.p037.s002: In siRNA Wizard 3.1, the 128 nt-long B. tabaci hsp70 fragment produced a putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GAUCCAUCCAUGCCGUUAAUC-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s003: The 150 nt-long fas2 fragment also yielded one putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GGACGGAAGAAGGCAUAAUCA-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s004: The dsRNA sequence was specific to B. tabaci, and no cross-reactivity was recorded with other organisms such as Homo sapiens, Mus musculus, Aves (taxid:8782), Lepidoptera (taxid:7088), Hymenoptera (taxid:7399), Formicidae (taxid:36668), and plants (taxid:3193) in BLAST analysis.

events.event_label: feeding decreases expression of hsp70 and fas2
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p040.s003: The mRNA expression levels were significantly reduced with an increase in hsp70 dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p040.s004: An exposure to hsp70 dsRNA at 2.0 μg/mL for 24 h significantly reduced the hsp70 mRNA expression by 11.32-fold and up to 12.85-fold when fed with 3.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p040.s005: The fas2 mRNA expression levels were declined by 5.48-fold post-24 h feeding on fas2 dsRNA at 1.0 μg/mL. The mRNA expression levels post-hsp70 and -fas2 dsRNA feeding were almost similar at 1.0 μg/mL. The mRNA expression levels were significantly reduced up to 8.53-fold with an increase in fas2 dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p040.s006: The reduction in mRNA expression level was significantly higher post-hsp70 dsRNA feeding compared to fas2 dsRNA at 2.0 and 3.0 μg/mL. The primer pairs for hsp70, fas2, and β-actin did not produce any secondary peaks in the RT-qPCR melting curve analysis that indicated the specificity of the reactions (Figure 2d,e).

events.event_label: dsRNA inhibits transmission of ChiLCV
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p043.s006: However, the severity of the leaf curl symptoms in inoculated plants was less than the untreated control. PMC8909865.p043.s007: The transmission of ChiLCV was decreased to 40% when B. tabaci were fed with 2.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA for 24 h (Figure 3e). PMC8909865.p043.s008: The inoculated plants that tested positive in PCR produced mild leaf curl symptoms. PMC8909865.p043.s009: Interestingly, there was no transmission of ChiLCV when B. tabaci was exposed to 3.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p043.s010: All the inoculated plants were without any leaf curl symptoms (Figure 3f) and tested ChiLCV-free in PCR at 35 days post-inoculation.

events.event_label: dsRNA exposure increases mortality of B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p052.s015: Similar types of artificial feeding setup were reported to be efficient in the oral delivery of dsRNA. PMC8909865.p052.s016: A significant knockdown of the B. tabaci population was recorded at 24 h, which gradually increased with an increase in dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p052.s017: Up to 82.22% mortality of B. tabaci was recorded after 24 h feeding on 3.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p052.s018: Similarly, fas2 dsRNA at 3.0 μg/mL caused 72% mortality of B. tabaci 24 h post-feeding. PMC8909865.p052.s019: The dsRNA exposure downregulated the hsp70 and fas2 mRNA expressions by 12.85- and 8.53-fold, respectively.

events.event_label: dsRNA spray effective under semi-field conditions and suitable for field use
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p055.s006: Infection of ChiLCV at the seedling stage leads to a considerable reduction in plant height, internodal length, and fruiting. PMC8909865.p055.s007: Hence, a 20-day shield by 2 consecutive sprays of naked hsp70 dsRNA would protect the crucial phase of crop growth from the invasion of B. tabaci and ChiLCV and reduce yield losses. PMC8909865.p055.s008: The foliar spray of hsp70 dsRNA was effective under semi-field conditions where the daily temperature fluctuated between 20–35 °C with 40–60% RH. PMC8909865.p055.s009: The efficacy and persistency of topically sprayed naked dsRNA at a wide temperature range endorse its potentiality for field uses as a spray-on technology.

events.event_label: dsRNA targeting hsp70 and fas2 induces mortality and reduces target gene expression
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p002.s001: Chilli leaf curl virus (ChiLCV; genus: Begomovirus), transmitted by Bemisia tabaci (Gennadius) (Hemiptera: Aleyrodidae) in a persistent-circulative manner, is a major constraint in chilli production. PMC8909865.p002.s002: The present study demonstrates for the first time that a topical spray of naked double-stranded RNA (dsRNA) on chilli plants causes mortality and inability to acquire and transmit ChiLCV in B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p002.s003: dsRNA targeting heat shock protein 70 (hsp70) and fasciclin 2 (fas2) of B. tabaci Asia II 1 was first assessed under controlled conditions through oral delivery. PMC8909865.p002.s004: Hsp70 and fas2 dsRNA resulted in up to 82.22% and 72% mortality of B. tabaci and around 12.4- and 8.5-fold decreases in mRNA levels, respectively, 24 h post-ingestion. PMC8909865.p002.s005: ChiLCV copies in hsp70 dsRNA-fed B. tabaci steadily decreased with an increase in dsRNA concentration and were undetectable at a higher concentration of dsRNA. PMC8909865.p002.s006: However, ChiLCV copies significantly increased in fas2 dsRNA-fed B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p002.s007: Transmission of ChiLCV by B. tabaci was completely inhibited post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA at 3 μg/mL. Naked hsp70 dsRNA was topically sprayed on ChiLCV-infected chilli plants like an insecticide.

PMC8909865.p052.s006: The fas2 mRNA expression level was upregulated in viruliferous B. tabaci by 2.6 and 4.518-fold in the RNA-Seq and RT-qPCR assay, respectively. PMC8909865.p052.s007: The Fas2 orthologue in mammals (NCAM) is known to serve as a receptor for the rabies virus.

events.event_label: Topical dsRNA spray affects mortality and transmission of ChiLCV
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p160.s001: Effect of topical spray of naked hsp70 dsRNA on mortality and ChiLCV transmission by Bemisia tabaci. PMC8909865.p160.s002: dsRNA was not directly applied on B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p160.s003: The naked hsp70 dsRNA was sprayed onto the foliage of ChiLCV-infected chilli plants like an insecticide at 30 μg/mL. B. tabaci adults were released on the dsRNA-treated plants. PMC8909865.p160.s004: (a) Percent mortality of adult B. tabaci post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA-treated plants.

events.event_label: silencing hsp70 and fas2 affects mortality and virus transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p006.s003: The objective of the present study was to induce resistance against B. tabaci and ChiLCV by spray-on application of dsRNA. PMC8909865.p006.s004: In the present study, B. tabaci hsp70 and fas2 were silenced and resultant effects in mortality and virus transmission were reported. PMC8909865.p006.s005: For the first time, the efficacy of spray-on application of naked dsRNA against a hemipteran insect was demonstrated under semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p006.s006: The outcomes of the study provide a novel, eco-friendly option for protection against B. tabaci as well as ChiLCV, which can reduce economic losses caused by the virus-vector complex.

events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p052.s006: The fas2 mRNA expression level was upregulated in viruliferous B. tabaci by 2.6 and 4.518-fold in the RNA-Seq and RT-qPCR assay, respectively. PMC8909865.p052.s007: The Fas2 orthologue in mammals (NCAM) is known to serve as a receptor for the rabies virus. events.event_label: insecticides associated with B. tabaci and ChiLCV
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p051.s001: Several management strategies have been adopted around the world, with insecticides as the core component for the management of the B. tabaci-ChiLCV complex. PMC8909865.p051.s002: However, the use of chemical insecticides has led to several detrimental effects like the development of insecticide-resistant B. tabaci population, destruction of beneficial organisms, environmental contamination, and health hazards. PMC8909865.p051.s003: There is a need for an alternative eco-friendly approach in managing B. tabaci-ChiLCV. PMC8909865.p051.s004: RNAi or post-transcriptional gene silencing (PTGS) was introduced to study the functionality of specific genes through knocking down target gene mRNA.

events.event_label: mtCOI gene detected in B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p034.s001: A homogeneous population of B. tabaci developed from a single adult female was characterized by sequencing the mtCOI gene. PMC8909865.p034.s002: PCR amplification of B. tabaci mtCOI with C1-J-2195 and L2-N-3014 primers (Table 1) showed an expected amplicon of ~860 bp on an agarose gel. PMC8909865.p034.s003: The nucleotide (nt) sequence showed 99.99% homology in BLASTn analysis with other B. tabaci Asia II 1 sequences in NCBI. PMC8909865.p034.s004: The sequence can be retrieved using the GenBank Accession No. MT920041.

events.event_label: dsRNA increases mortality of B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p039.s003: Mortality of 67.77% of B. tabaci was observed at 24 h upon feeding on 1.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p039.s004: The mortality of B. tabaci significantly increased with an increase in dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p039.s005: The mean mortality was 70.0% when hsp70 dsRNA was fed to B. tabaci adults at 2.0 μg/mL, and it increased significantly up to 82.22% at 3.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA (Figure 2b). PMC8909865.p039.s006: Oral delivery of fas2 dsRNA at 1.0 μg/mL exhibited 39.33% mean mortality after 24 h (Figure 2b). PMC8909865.p039.s007: Significantly higher mortality (62 and 72%) of B. tabaci adults was recorded when 2.0 and 3.0 μg/mL fas2 dsRNA was orally delivered to B. tabaci under controlled laboratory conditions. PMC8909865.p039.s008: At all doses, the mortality of B. tabaci post-fas2 dsRNA exposure was significantly lower than the hsp70 dsRNA at p < 0.05. PMC8909865.p039.s009: However, no morpho-deformities were observed in killed B. tabaci adults either with hsp70 or fas2 dsRNA.

events.event_label: dsRNA knockdown of fas2 reduces fas2 expression and causes B. tabaci mortality
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p002.s001: Chilli leaf curl virus (ChiLCV; genus: Begomovirus), transmitted by Bemisia tabaci (Gennadius) (Hemiptera: Aleyrodidae) in a persistent-circulative manner, is a major constraint in chilli production. PMC8909865.p002.s002: The present study demonstrates for the first time that a topical spray of naked double-stranded RNA (dsRNA) on chilli plants causes mortality and inability to acquire and transmit ChiLCV in B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p002.s003: dsRNA targeting heat shock protein 70 (hsp70) and fasciclin 2 (fas2) of B. tabaci Asia II 1 was first assessed under controlled conditions through oral delivery. PMC8909865.p002.s004: Hsp70 and fas2 dsRNA resulted in up to 82.22% and 72% mortality of B. tabaci and around 12.4- and 8.5-fold decreases in mRNA levels, respectively, 24 h post-ingestion. PMC8909865.p002.s005: ChiLCV copies in hsp70 dsRNA-fed B. tabaci steadily decreased with an increase in dsRNA concentration and were undetectable at a higher concentration of dsRNA. PMC8909865.p002.s006: However, ChiLCV copies significantly increased in fas2 dsRNA-fed B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p002.s007: Transmission of ChiLCV by B. tabaci was completely inhibited post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA at 3 μg/mL. Naked hsp70 dsRNA was topically sprayed on ChiLCV-infected chilli plants like an insecticide.

events.event_label: feeding on hsp70 dsRNA decreases hsp70 expression
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p040.s001: RT-qPCR analysis showed that hsp70 dsRNA significantly reduced the hsp70 mRNA expression levels in treated B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p040.s002: Hsp70 mRNA levels in adult B. tabaci were decreased by 5.43-fold with respect to an endogenous control gene, β-actin, post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA (Figure 2c). PMC8909865.p040.s003: The mRNA expression levels were significantly reduced with an increase in hsp70 dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p040.s004: An exposure to hsp70 dsRNA at 2.0 μg/mL for 24 h significantly reduced the hsp70 mRNA expression by 11.32-fold and up to 12.85-fold when fed with 3.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p040.s005: The fas2 mRNA expression levels were declined by 5.48-fold post-24 h feeding on fas2 dsRNA at 1.0 μg/mL. The mRNA expression levels post-hsp70 and -fas2 dsRNA feeding were almost similar at 1.0 μg/mL. The mRNA expression levels were significantly reduced up to 8.53-fold with an increase in fas2 dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p040.s006: The reduction in mRNA expression level was significantly higher post-hsp70 dsRNA feeding compared to fas2 dsRNA at 2.0 and 3.0 μg/mL. The primer pairs for hsp70, fas2, and β-actin did not produce any secondary peaks in the RT-qPCR melting curve analysis that indicated the specificity of the reactions (Figure 2d,e).

events.event_label: dsRNA inhibits ChiLCV transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p054.s009: Hence, naked hsp70 dsRNA without any delivery agent was topically sprayed onto the foliage of the ChiLCV-infected chilli plant under contained semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p054.s010: B. tabaci adults were released on the dsRNA-sprayed plants to evaluate the dsRNA’s efficacy in eradicating B. tabaci and inhibiting the transmission of ChiLCV. PMC8909865.p054.s011: Considering the extracellular degradation of dsRNA and delivery through the host plant, a 10-fold higher dose than the controlled assay was used for topical application. PMC8909865.p054.s012: A 67.77% mortality with a 4.6-fold decrease in hsp70 mRNA levels in B. tabaci exposed to the dsRNA-sprayed plants was noted after 24 h of release. PMC8909865.p054.s013: Further, 1.84 × 108-fold decreased ChiLCV copies and 75% reduced transmission of ChiLCV indicated the efficiency of topically sprayed hsp70 dsRNA in managing both the virus and its vector. PMC8909865.p054.s014: Although the efficacy of topically applied naked dsRNA against plant viruses is known, this is the first evidence of its efficacy against a hemipteran insect like B. tabaci.

events.event_label: dsRNA detected in leaf tissues and confers tolerance under semi‑field conditions
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p055.s001: Topical application of dsRNA could provide resistance for 12 to 21 days against plant viruses. PMC8909865.p055.s002: In the present study, dsRNA was detected in the leaf tissues up to 6 h post-application. PMC8909865.p055.s003: Significant mortality of B. tabaci on the treated plants persisted up to 8 days and was further sustained up to 20 days with a second spray on the 10th day. PMC8909865.p055.s004: Protection of the plants during the early growth stage is crucial in the management of begomoviruses. PMC8909865.p055.s006: Infection of ChiLCV at the seedling stage leads to a considerable reduction in plant height, internodal length, and fruiting. PMC8909865.p055.s007: Hence, a 20-day shield by 2 consecutive sprays of naked hsp70 dsRNA would protect the crucial phase of crop growth from the invasion of B. tabaci and ChiLCV and reduce yield losses. PMC8909865.p055.s008: The foliar spray of hsp70 dsRNA was effective under semi-field conditions where the daily temperature fluctuated between 20–35 °C with 40–60% RH. PMC8909865.p055.s009: The efficacy and persistency of topically sprayed naked dsRNA at a wide temperature range endorse its potentiality for field uses as a spray-on technology.

events.event_label: fas2 is expressed in B. tabaci and affects the insect
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p053.s021: The Fas2 orthologue in mammals (NCAM) is known to serve as a receptor for the rabies virus. PMC8909865.p053.s022: Although NCAM promotes the penetration of the virus in cells, it suppresses virus replication via induction of Interferon-ß, which is mainly involved in innate immunity against viral infection. PMC8909865.p053.s023: Upregulation of fas2 transcripts in B. tabaci post-ChiLCV infection might be due to the innate immune response against the virus infection. PMC8909865.p053.s024: We hypothesized that fas2 has a negative regulatory role in ChiLCV infection, and thus, knocking down fas2 increased the virus titer in B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p053.s025: Hence, silencing fas2 was not considered to induce resistance to ChiLCV despite its efficacy in eradicating B. tabaci.

events.event_label: hsp70 and fas2 fragments generate dsRNA
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p037.s001: A conserved 128 nt-long (2416 to 2543 nt) fragment of the hsp70 gene (~2.5 kb) and a 150 nt-long (1085 to 1234 nt) fragment of the fas2 gene (~1.4 kb) of B. tabaci were selected for dsRNA designing (Figure 1a,b). PMC8909865.p037.s002: In siRNA Wizard 3.1, the 128 nt-long B. tabaci hsp70 fragment produced a putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GAUCCAUCCAUGCCGUUAAUC-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s003: The 150 nt-long fas2 fragment also yielded one putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GGACGGAAGAAGGCAUAAUCA-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s004: The dsRNA sequence was specific to B. tabaci, and no cross-reactivity was recorded with other organisms such as Homo sapiens, Mus musculus, Aves (taxid:8782), Lepidoptera (taxid:7088), Hymenoptera (taxid:7399), Formicidae (taxid:36668), and plants (taxid:3193) in BLAST analysis. PMC8909865.p037.s006: The fragments were cloned between two T7 promoters of L4440 RNAi vector (Addgene 1654 provided by Andrew Fire, Carnegie Institution for Science, Washington, DC, USA). PMC8909865.p037.s007: The recombinant plasmids were transformed into RNase III mutant Escherichia coli HT115 cells (provided by Caenorhabditis Genetics Center, Minneapolis, MN, USA). PMC8909865.p037.s008: Total RNA isolated from recombinant E. coli HT115 cells and the dsRNA purified from total RNA using DNase I and RNase A were visualized in agarose gel electrophoresis (Figure 1c). PMC8909865.p037.s009: The purified dsRNA of hsp70 and fas2 produced single specific bands of ~130 bp and ~150 bp on 2% native agarose gel (Figure 1c). PMC8909865.p037.s010: Upon bidirectional sequencing of the recombinant plasmids, both the sequences of hsp70 (Accession No. MZ158306) and fas2 (Accession No. MZ766125) showed 100% homology with B. tabaci hsp70 and fas2, respectively.

PMC8909865.p002.s010: A total of 2 consecutive sprays of dsRNA provided significant protection to B. tabaci for up to 20 days under semi-field conditions.

events.event_label: feeding occurs_in Bemisia tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p004.s001: Silverleaf whitefly [Bemisia tabaci (Gennadius), Hemiptera: Aleyrodidae] is a phloem-feeding hemipteran insect that has been reported to infest over 600 plant species worldwide. PMC8909865.p004.s002: B. tabaci has been considered a complex of at least 46 morphologically indistinguishable cryptic species. PMC8909865.p004.s003: B. tabaci adults are about 1 mm long with small triangular-shaped bodies. PMC8909865.p004.s004: The body is yellowish with distinct hyaline wings dusted with white powdery wax.

events.event_label: honeydew affects sooty mold fungus
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p004.s006: The wings are held close to the body and tent-like in B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p004.s007: It sucks phloem sap, leaving the affected plants extremely weak, and secretes a honeydew onto the surface of the leaves that promotes the growth of sooty mold fungus. PMC8909865.p004.s008: Besides direct damage caused by feeding, B. tabaci transmits more than a hundred begomoviruses, carlaviruses, criniviruses, cytorhabdoviruses, ipomoviruses, poleroviruses, and torradoviruses (. PMC8909865.p004.s009: Among them, begomoviruses represent 90% of the viruses transmitted by B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p004.s010: Begomovirus species have become widespread in Central America, the Dominican Republic, Israel, Mexico, Trinidad, and across South East Asia, including Cambodia, Indonesia, India, and Thailand.

events.event_label: insecticides affects B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p005.s009: Yield losses of 20–50% have been recorded in chilli, which may rise to 100% in the case of simultaneous infestation with thrips and mites. PMC8909865.p005.s010: Control options for ChiLCV and B. tabaci are very limited as insecticides continue to lose their efficacy due to the emergence of insecticide-resistant B. tabaci populations. PMC8909865.p005.s011: Besides, insecticides adversely affect the environment and human health. PMC8909865.p005.s012: Considering the continued lack of consumer acceptance, transgenic plant technology is not a feasible strategy. PMC8909865.p005.s013: In recent years, RNA interference (RNAi) has shown promise in the management of insect pests and plant viruses.

relations.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p037.s001: A conserved 128 nt-long (2416 to 2543 nt) fragment of the hsp70 gene (~2.5 kb) and a 150 nt-long (1085 to 1234 nt) fragment of the fas2 gene (~1.4 kb) of B. tabaci were selected for dsRNA designing (Figure 1a,b). PMC8909865.p037.s002: In siRNA Wizard 3.1, the 128 nt-long B. tabaci hsp70 fragment produced a putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GAUCCAUCCAUGCCGUUAAUC-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s003: The 150 nt-long fas2 fragment also yielded one putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GGACGGAAGAAGGCAUAAUCA-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s004: The dsRNA sequence was specific to B. tabaci, and no cross-reactivity was recorded with other organisms such as Homo sapiens, Mus musculus, Aves (taxid:8782), Lepidoptera (taxid:7088), Hymenoptera (taxid:7399), Formicidae (taxid:36668), and plants (taxid:3193) in BLAST analysis.

relations.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p039.s003: Mortality of 67.77% of B. tabaci was observed at 24 h upon feeding on 1.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p039.s004: The mortality of B. tabaci significantly increased with an increase in dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p039.s005: The mean mortality was 70.0% when hsp70 dsRNA was fed to B. tabaci adults at 2.0 μg/mL, and it increased significantly up to 82.22% at 3.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA (Figure 2b). PMC8909865.p039.s006: Oral delivery of fas2 dsRNA at 1.0 μg/mL exhibited 39.33% mean mortality after 24 h (Figure 2b). PMC8909865.p039.s007: Significantly higher mortality (62 and 72%) of B. tabaci adults was recorded when 2.0 and 3.0 μg/mL fas2 dsRNA was orally delivered to B. tabaci under controlled laboratory conditions.

evidence.text: In the present study, for the first time, we demonstrated the efficacy of a naked dsRNA against an insect pest by spray-on application under semi-field conditions.
The dsRNA construct targeting the hsp70 gene of B. tabaci not only induced mortality in B. tabaci but also inhibited the transmission of ChiLCV from infected to healthy chilli plants by B. tabaci.

evidence.text: A homogeneous isofemale line of B. tabaci was raised from a single adult female and has been maintained on eggplants (var. Navkiran, Mahyco) at the Advanced Centre for Plant Virology, Indian Agricultural Research Institute (IARI), New Delhi, since 2015. The population was characterized by sequencing the mitochondrial cytochrome oxidase subunit I (mtCOI) gene (Table 1). The genotype or cryptic species of the B. tabaci population was confirmed based on Bayesian Inference phylogeny, considering a genetic divergence cutoff of 4% as described by Rehman et al.. The population was maintained under controlled environmental conditions at 28 ± 2 °C, 60 ± 10% relative humidity, and a 16 h light-8 h dark photoperiod.

entities.selected_description: Any biological process that results in permanent cessation of all vital functions of a cell. A cell should be considered dead when any one of the following molecular or morphological criteria is met: (1) the cell has lost the integrity of its plasma membrane; (2) the cell, including its nucleus, has undergone complete fragmentation into discrete bodies (frequently referred to as apoptotic bodies). The cell corpse (or its fragments) may be engulfed by an adjacent cell in vivo, but engulfment of whole cells should not be considered a strict criteria to define cell death as, under some circumstances, live engulfed cells can be released from phagosomes (see PMID:18045538).

sentences.text: Topical Spray of dsRNA Induces Mortality and Inhibits Chilli Leaf Curl Virus Transmission by Bemisia tabaci Asia II 1
Chilli leaf curl virus (ChiLCV; genus: Begomovirus), transmitted by Bemisia tabaci (Gennadius) (Hemiptera: Aleyrodidae) in a persistent-circulative manner, is a major constraint in chilli production.
The present study demonstrates for the first time that a topical spray of naked double-stranded RNA (dsRNA) on chilli plants causes mortality and inability to acquire and transmit ChiLCV in B.
tabaci.
dsRNA targeting heat shock protein 70 (hsp70) and fasciclin 2 (fas2) of B.
tabaci Asia II 1 was first assessed under controlled conditions through oral delivery.
Hsp70 and fas2 dsRNA resulted in up to 82.
22% and 72% mortality of B.

Silverleaf whitefly [Bemisia tabaci (Gennadius), Hemiptera: Aleyrodidae] is a phloem-feeding hemipteran insect that has been reported to infest over 600 plant species worldwide.
B.
tabaci has been considered a complex of at least 46 morphologically indistinguishable cryptic species.
B.
The body is yellowish with distinct hyaline wings dusted with white powdery wax.
Adults B.
tabaci can be distinguished from other whiteflies by the position in which the wings are held over the body.
The wings are held close to the body and tent-like in B.
tabaci.
ChiLCV has been responsible for several epidemics in India and Sri Lanka.

The disease is typically manifested in the infected plants as upward curling, puckering, bunching of leaves, blistering of interveinal areas, thickening, and swelling of the veins, shortening of internodes and petioles, and stunting of the whole plant.
The leaves become smaller and severely affected plants produce fewer and deformed fruits.
Yield losses of 20–50% have been recorded in chilli, which may rise to 100% in the case of simultaneous infestation with thrips and mites.
Control options for ChiLCV and B.
The objective of the present study was to induce resistance against B.
tabaci and ChiLCV by spray-on application of dsRNA.
In the present study, B.

tabaci hsp70 and fas2 were silenced and resultant effects in mortality and virus transmission were reported. For the first time, the efficacy of spray-on application of naked dsRNA against a hemipteran insect was demonstrated under semi-field conditions.

Several management strategies have been adopted around the world, with insecticides as the core component for the management of the B.
tabaci-ChiLCV complex.
However, the use of chemical insecticides has led to several detrimental effects like the development of insecticide-resistant B.
tabaci population, destruction of beneficial organisms, environmental contamination, and health hazards.
With the advancements in next-generation sequencing and transcriptomics technology, several genes have been identified as potential targets to control B.

Although RNAi has emerged as a promising alternative to suppress crop pests, there are limitations in the application of dsRNA as a ‘spray-on’ technology for field use.
Reduced stability in the extracellular environments of dsRNA contributes to poor RNAi response.
The naked dsRNA quickly degrades in higher temperatures and UV radiation under natural environmental conditions.
Besides, several insect species, mainly lepidopteran insects, have been observed to be refractory to RNAi due to degradation and poor intracellular transport of exogenous dsRNA.
Adult B.

tabaci are highly mobile, so spraying on adult flies was not preferred. Hence, naked hsp70 dsRNA without any delivery agent was topically sprayed onto the foliage of the ChiLCV-infected chilli plant under contained semi-field conditions.
B.
tabaci adults were released on the dsRNA-sprayed plants to evaluate the dsRNA’s efficacy in eradicating B.
tabaci and inhibiting the transmission of ChiLCV.
Considering the extracellular degradation of dsRNA and delivery through the host plant, a 10-fold higher dose than the controlled assay was used for topical application.
Topical application of dsRNA could provide resistance for 12 to 21 days against plant viruses.
In the present study, dsRNA was detected in the leaf tissues up to 6 h post-application.
Significant mortality of B.
tabaci on the treated plants persisted up to 8 days and was further sustained up to 20 days with a second spray on the 10th day.
Begomovirus infection at a 5-leaf stage in tomatoes reduced yield by 95%.
Infection of ChiLCV at the seedling stage leads to a considerable reduction in plant height, internodal length, and fruiting.
Hence, a 20-day shield by 2 consecutive sprays of naked hsp70 dsRNA would protect the crucial phase of crop growth from the invasion of B.
tabaci and ChiLCV and reduce yield losses.
